tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal odor, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, glucosuria) further support possible renal and/or hepatic dysfunction. HbA1c is low, indicating no chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further evaluation of renal and hepatic status.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
